request.question: Conjugating doxorubicin to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles will increase intracellular drug accumulation in HeLa cells by at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin, due to folate receptor-mediated endocytosis overexpressed in cancer cells, while reducing cytotoxicity in folate receptor-negative HEK293 cells by at least 40%.

hypothesis.intervention: conjugating doxorubicin to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles
hypothesis.threshold: at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin
hypothesis.mechanistic_rationale: folate receptor-mediated endocytosis overexpressed in cancer cells
hypothesis.readiness_rationale: All core fields are present and specific, making the hypothesis CRO-ready today.
hypothesis.literature_search_hint: doxorubicin folate PLGA nanoparticles HeLa intracellular accumulation

plan.objective: To determine the intracellular accumulation of doxorubicin when conjugated to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles in HeLa cells compared to free doxorubicin.
plan.experimental_design: This experiment will compare the intracellular drug accumulation of doxorubicin delivered via folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles against free doxorubicin in HeLa cells. The key gating logic involves measuring the fluorescence intensity of doxorubicin within the cells to quantify drug accumulation after treatment with both formulations.
controls: Free doxorubicin at a concentration of 1 µM.
Untreated HeLa cells without any doxorubicin treatment.

step_by_step_protocol.description: Prepare folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles by dissolving 100 mg of PLGA in 5 mL of dichloromethane. Add 10 mg of folate and stir for 2 hours at room temperature. Remove the solvent under reduced pressure to obtain a dry polymer. Resuspend the polymer in 5 mL of PBS and sonicate for 10 minutes to form nanoparticles. Characterize the nanoparticles using dynamic light scattering (DLS) to confirm size and zeta potential.

Conjugate doxorubicin to the prepared folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles. Dissolve 5 mg of doxorubicin in 1 mL of DMSO and add it to the nanoparticle suspension. Incubate for 4 hours at room temperature with gentle stirring. Purify the conjugated nanoparticles using centrifugation at 14,000 rpm for 30 minutes and resuspend in PBS.
Culture HeLa cells in 6-well plates at a density of 1 x 10^5 cells/well in complete DMEM medium. Incubate overnight at 37°C with 5% CO2 to allow for cell attachment.

Treat HeLa cells with either free doxorubicin (1 µM) or doxorubicin-conjugated folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles (equivalent doxorubicin concentration of 1 µM) for 4 hours. Include untreated cells as a negative control. After treatment, wash cells with PBS and lyse with 500 µL of lysis buffer. Measure fluorescence intensity at 480 nm excitation and 590 nm emission using a fluorescence plate reader.

Analyze the fluorescence data to quantify intracellular drug accumulation. Compare the fluorescence intensity of cells treated with doxorubicin-conjugated nanoparticles to those treated with free doxorubicin. Calculate the fold increase in drug accumulation and assess if it meets the threshold of at least 3-fold increase.

assumptions: Folate receptors are overexpressed in HeLa cells, allowing for effective uptake of folate-functionalized nanoparticles.
Doxorubicin conjugation does not significantly alter the drug's efficacy or fluorescence properties.
The fluorescence intensity measured correlates directly with the amount of doxorubicin accumulated intracellularly.
decision_criteria: If the intracellular accumulation of doxorubicin from nanoparticles is at least 3-fold higher than that from free doxorubicin, proceed to further studies.
If the accumulation is less than 3-fold, reassess the nanoparticle formulation and dosing strategy.
If there are significant variations in fluorescence readings, repeat the experiment to confirm results.
reproducibility_notes: Record the lot numbers of all reagents used, including PLGA and doxorubicin.
Standardize the cell passage number and culture conditions for HeLa cells across experiments.
Document the exact conditions (temperature, time, and concentrations) for all treatments.

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: To determine the intracellular accumulation of doxorubicin when conjugated to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles in HeLa cells compared to free doxorubicin.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
